fields.description:
## Why

Epic A must EXPERIMENTALLY PROVE the new constructs and DE-RISK the epic-B plan-review migration —
finding flaws in our implementation and resolving them as A-scope, so B is a migration, not a
discovery. A trivial smoke test does not do that; a realistic vertical slice of the actual B
pipeline does.

## What

Author a throwaway but REALISTIC 4-pass review SKELETON workflow (the shape B migrates onto) and run
it OFFLINE under FakeAgentRunner — no live calls — to surface integration flaws now:
- overlay_triggers (A1) precompute -> a `batch` finder over a `criteria` list (each criterion a
  prompt-library-backed entry with an optional `when` overlay; A2 shape) -> ONE aggregate verify
  prompt -> a `uses` decide step.
- Capture a PLANNED-TRACE from the run — the ordered [criterion/prompt-id, intended model, call-mode
  (agent|1-shot), deterministic-step-id] — proving epic B's parity-validation mechanism is actually
  emittable from the engine BEFORE B depends on it (the single biggest B risk).
Treat discovered flaws (can the criteria list express conditional inclusion? does the runner journal
a usable plan? is the planned-trace capturable? does replay hold?) as work to FIX here.
Label the workflow DISPOSABLE — epic B removes it, retaining it meanwhile as the visual-editing sample.

## Scope

- src/rebar/llm/workflow/examples/<demo>.yaml (clearly labeled throwaway): the realistic skeleton.
- A test (tests/unit/workflow/) running it under FakeAgentRunner: asserts conditional criteria are
  included/skipped, the batch-plan is journaled, the aggregate verify + decide run, and a PLANNED
  TRACE is captured — all offline, zero live calls.
- Record the flaws found + how they were resolved (the de-risking outcome).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A realistic offline 4-pass skeleton (overlay_triggers -> batch finder over a prompt-backed
      criteria list with per-criterion `when` -> aggregate verify -> decide) runs end-to-end under
      FakeAgentRunner with zero live calls.
- [ ] A PLANNED-TRACE is captured from the run (criterion/prompt-id + intended model + call-mode +
      deterministic steps), demonstrating epic B's validation mechanism is emittable from the engine.
- [ ] The experiment surfaces + the ticket records any construct flaws found, and they are FIXED as
      part of epic A (not deferred to B).
- [ ] The workflow is labeled disposable (removed by epic B; meanwhile the visual-editing sample);
      make lint + the workflow test suite pass.
